purpose: Non-circular benchmark for trusty-search hybrid retrieval (issue #123).

categories.definition: Single distinctive identifier — caller wants the file where it is defined.
categories.conceptual: Multi-word phrase describing what the code does, not the identifier itself.
categories.usage: Caller wants files that USE a given API, not the file that defines it.
categories.data: Query targets the config.yaml structured-data file (mode=data).

queries.notes: Hammond appears only via HAMMOND_TICK_RATE consumption in LichtenbergCascade. Tests whether snake_case (constants.rs) vs PascalCase ranking surfaces the engine site that uses it.
Exact PascalCase struct name. The trivial-positive: hybrid mode should hit-at-1.
Snake_case function name. Tests #119 fix (snake_case Definition routing).
SCREAMING_SNAKE constant. Tests acronym/constant Definition routing.
Multi-word description of what the engine does. Vector lane should win.

queries.text: flatten clustered tree into depth first vector
queries.notes: Conceptual description of yamamoto flatten. No exact identifier match.

queries.text: fixed point iteration with damping factor
queries.notes: Describes the inner solver step. Hybrid should fuse on damping/iteration.
Two-file ground truth — either cipher.rs or codec.rs counts as hit.
Usage sites of BrusilovTransform, NOT its definition site. Tests Usage routing (KG lane).
Single caller — pipeline.rs. Tests if KG-leading retrieval finds the call site.
README content query. Tests mode=text routing.

queries.text: changelog initial release notes synthetic corpus
queries.notes: CHANGELOG content query. Tests mode=text + filename-aware ranking.

queries.text: configured outbound channels for archive and dashboard
queries.notes: Targets the maltesian.registered_channels section of config.yaml.

queries.text: seraphim damping factor and iteration cap
queries.notes: Operator config + constants both legitimately answer. config.yaml is the primary target.
